summary: Install-time exfiltration via binding.gyp command-expansion to ngrok endpoint
details: On npm install, node-gyp rebuild is triggered by a shipped binding.gyp whose 'sources' field uses GYP command-expansion syntax (<!(node index.js...)) to execute index.js during the configure step. The package ships no C/C++ source files, so binding.gyp exists solely to run the embedded command. index.js opens an https.request POST to the hardcoded host crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev carrying os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and process.env.GITHUB_REPOSITORY, along with a literal proof marker string 'GYP_BYPASS_IGNORE_SCRIPTS_SUCCESS' — indicating the author's intent to execute even when the installer runs npm install with --ignore-scripts. Result: any environment installing this package leaks host identity, OS username, and GitHub repository context to an attacker-controlled ngrok tunnel at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
